task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the 'Requirement' column (e.g., 'REQ-SYS-01: Pulse Width Accuracy') has a gray background shading indicating it is an active field/cross-reference, OR there are screenshots showing the agent using the 'Insert -> Cross-reference' dialog box to add these headings.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI word processor (like LibreOffice Writer) to edit the document
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show LibreOffice Writer (or a similar GUI application) open and being interacted with, rather than terminal commands modifying the ODT file directly via scripts.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual cross-reference feature rather than faking it
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen interacting with the cross-reference insertion dialog, rather than just typing the text and manually applying a gray background color to fake the field shading.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script or CLI tool to bypass GUI interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal does not show the execution of a script (e.g., python script using odfpy) to generate the NeuroStim_SRS_v2.odt file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct mappings from trace_data.json are: REQ-SYS-01 -> VP-055 (Bench Test); REQ-SYS-02 -> VP-056 (Analysis); REQ-SYS-03 -> VP-088 (Field Test); REQ-SYS-04 -> VP-012 (Helium Leak Test); REQ-SYS-05 -> VP-099 (Safety Inspection). The final file must be named 'NeuroStim_SRS_v2.odt'. In LibreOffice, active cross-references typically display with a gray field shading by default.
